steps.title: Recognize AA similarity criterion
steps.content: We have ∠A = ∠P = 52°. For AA similarity, two pairs of equal angles are sufficient. We need to find one more matching angle pair.

steps.title: Test Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 40° and 88°, respectively.
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 40° and 88°, respectively. (∠B = 40°, ∠R = 88°):

In triangle ABC with ∠A = 52°, ∠B = 40°:
∠C = 180° - 52° - 40° = 88°
Angles: 52°, 40°, 88°

In triangle PQR with ∠P = 52°, ∠R = 88°:
∠Q = 180° - 52° - 88° = 40°
Angles: 52°, 40°, 88°

All angles match ✓

steps.title: Confirm similarity
steps.content: Both triangles have angles 52°, 40°, 88°. With ∠A = ∠P and ∠B = ∠Q, the AA criterion is satisfied, so △ABC ∼ △PQR. That matches Choice C — The measures of angle B and angle R are 40° and 88°, respectively..
